title: Versioning, Delete Markers, Object Lock, and Recovery · Go Semantics Case: Restore Drill Evidence
summary: A team enables versioning after an incident, assumes delete markers are backups, and applies compliance retention without testing irreversible governance and restore procedures. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the restore drill evidence increment in version and immutable-retention recovery runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Run sampled restore drills with authorization, known bytes, metadata, dependencies, time objectives, audit evidence, and safe cleanup.
Separate WORM retention, version recovery, replication, independent backup, restore testing, and account-compromise boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Restore Drill Evidence · Theory Restore Drill Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 2177 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate version identity, immutable retention meaning, and executed restore evidence; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Run sampled restore drills with authorization, known bytes, metadata, dependencies, time objectives, audit evidence, and safe cleanup.
